mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce equity discount rates and lower borrowing costs for corporations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below the 6.5 percent threshold signals labor market tightening that triggers Federal Reserve policy normalization, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in residential construction spending and housing starts indicates a synchronized credit and real economy expansion that lifts corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing maximum thresholds, triggering supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and generate negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum during late-cycle phases produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 2.0 percent annualized threshold forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a direct proxy for expanding consumer demand and corporate revenue streams, generating positive quarterly equity returns through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and residential construction spending signals a shift from credit-driven to fundamentals-driven demand, reducing financial sector leverage and producing negative monthly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth outpaces RSAFS growth by > 1.0%
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable consumer prices indicates healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, supporting positive weekly equity returns during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO and CPIAUCSL joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window during early-cycle phases.
